app.name: Harlem FloodEscape
app.tagline: East Harlem flood resiliency — El Barrio

firstrun.title: Welcome to Harlem FloodEscape
firstrun.body: This is a research prototype from the City University of New York. It is not an official emergency service. In an emergency, always call 911. For official alerts, use Notify NYC.

about.title: About this app
about.prototype: Harlem FloodEscape is a research prototype from CUNY, not an official emergency service. In an emergency call 911. For official alerts use Notify NYC.
about.purpose: Built as part of dissertation research on flash-flood risk and community resilience in East Harlem: warnings must be fast and hyperlocal (recorded floods reach peak depth in a median of 38 minutes), and community reports fill the gaps official data misses.
about.privacy: Your privacy: everything you enter (plan, contacts, reports, photos) stays on this device only. Nothing is uploaded. You can erase all data anytime in My Plan → Settings.
about.attribution: Richard Rivera, CUNY Graduate Center. Supported by the NOAA Cooperative Science Center for Earth System Sciences and Remote Sensing Technologies (CESSRST-II), Cooperative Agreement #NA22SEC4810016 (NOAA EPP/MSI). The statements, findings, and conclusions are those of the author and do not necessarily reflect the views of NOAA.
about.credits: Data: NYC FloodNet · NYC OpenData (311) · NOAA / National Weather Service · OpenStreetMap contributors · Community partners: Community Board 11, WE ACT for Environmental Justice, Maggie's Magic Garden.

WARNING.title: Warning
WARNING.desc: Flooding likely or beginning — act now

DANGER.title: Danger
DANGER.desc: Flooding in progress — move to safety

source.offline: Live data unavailable — showing last known state

reason.calmDefault: No active alerts or sensor flooding
reason.nwsWarning: National Weather Service Flash Flood Warning in effect
reason.nwsWatch: National Weather Service Flood Watch in effect
reason.sensorMinor: Nearby sensors reporting minor street flooding
reason.sensorDeep: Nearby sensor water depth above curb height
reason.sensorVeryDeep: Nearby sensor water depth above car-door height

demo.title: Demo scenarios
demo.intro: Replay real recorded flood events, or run a simulation of what a similar storm could look like in East Harlem.

oct30.name: The October Surge — Oct 30, 2025
oct30.desc: The single worst day in the record: 896 street-flooding complaints and 128 sensor floods across the city. Recorded data.

may20.name: The Jamaica Flash Flood — May 20, 2026
may20.desc: Five sensors went from dry to 32–46 inches within 12 minutes, around 7:20 PM. The deepest water ever recorded by FloodNet. Recorded data.

ehsim.name: What if it happened here? — East Harlem
ehsim.desc: The May 20 flood pattern transposed onto East Harlem's low-lying streets. A simulation for planning — not a real event.

map.title: Map

sensor.liveNote: Live sensor positions and status from FloodNet. Live water depth is not public — depths appear in replays.

map.offlineTiles: Map tiles unavailable offline — locations and routes still shown.
map.locateDenied: Location unavailable. Showing East Harlem center instead.
map.destinationRisk: Heads up: this destination has {risk} flood risk.

report.title: Report flooding
report.intro: Your report becomes a data point that helps protect the neighborhood. It takes about 10 seconds.

location.label: Location
location.why: Used only to place your report on the map. It stays on this device.
location.pin: Drop a pin on the map

photo.label: Photo (optional)
photo.note: Photos are resized and location metadata is removed before saving.
photo.storageFull: Photo storage is full — the oldest photo was removed to make room.

report.safety: Never enter floodwater to take a photo. Report only what you can see from a safe place.
report.confirmBody: Your report is on the map. In the full program, calibrated community reports like this one would be shared with emergency managers (SCI protocol) — turning residents into the sensors East Harlem lacks.
report.clearConfirm: Delete all reports stored on this device?

plan.title: My Plan
plan.intro: A few minutes on a calm day is the cheapest flood protection there is.

risk.title: My block's flood risk
risk.explain: Drop a pin on your block to estimate risk from elevation and mapped flood-prone areas.
risk.resultLow: Lower risk: your block sits about {ft} ft above sea level and is not in a mapped flood-prone area.
risk.resultModerate: Moderate risk: your block sits about {ft} ft above sea level. Water can still pool in heavy rain.
risk.resultHigh: Higher risk: your block sits about {ft} ft above sea level and is in or near a mapped flood-prone area.
risk.disclaimer: Estimate for research demonstration — not a flood insurance or safety determination.

haven.title: My safe havens
haven.needLocation: Set your location above to get safe haven suggestions.

pet.title: Pet plan
pet.intro: Pets are family. Know where they can go before the water rises.

items.leash: Leash and collar with ID tag
items.records: Vaccination records (photo on your phone counts)
items.photo: A current photo of your pet

items.valuables: Move valuables and electronics up off the floor
items.shutoffs: Know your gas and electric shutoffs
items.neighbors: Check on elderly or disabled neighbors
items.drains: Clear the storm drain on your corner if safe

contacts.title: Household contacts
contacts.name: Name
contacts.max: Up to 5 contacts, stored only on this device.

settings.title: Settings
settings.eraseConfirm: This deletes your plan, contacts, reports, and photos from this device. There is no undo.

footer.line: Research prototype · CUNY Graduate Center · NOAA CESSRST-II #NA22SEC4810016 · In an emergency call 911
